title: Taiwan MND Detects 16 PLA Aircraft Sorties, 10 PLAN Vessels
detail: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 16 sorties of Chinese military aircraft, 10 PLAN vessels, and 2 official ships operating around Taiwan on March 25. Of the 16 sorties, 13 crossed the median line and entered Taiwan's southwestern and eastern ADIZ — a notable single-day spike compared to just 3 sorties the previous day. ROC Armed Forces monitored the situation and responded. Activity remains elevated ahead of the Trump-Xi summit, now postponed by 5–6 weeks due to the Iran conflict.

title: Taiwan Defense Minister: US Has 'High Urgency' in Speeding Arms Deliveries
detail: Taiwan's Defense Minister Wellington Koo stated that the United States has 'quite a high' sense of urgency in helping Taiwan strengthen its military capabilities and is working to speed up delayed weapons deliveries, including the 66 F-16V fighter jets ordered in 2019. A delegation led by Deputy Defense Minister Hsu Szu-chien visited Lockheed Martin's F-16 assembly line in South Carolina on March 16 and viewed the first completed aircraft. The US Defense Security Cooperation Agency director confirmed a 2023 directive prioritizing Taiwan above all other arms buyers remains in effect.
